title: Fun On A Bun

jokes.text: What? You double-dipped!
jokes.setup: Someone is disgusted by Fry's behavior at what appears to be a social gathering
jokes.punchline: The gross behavior is the mundane social faux pas of double-dipping
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment that establishes Fry's crudeness. Well-executed but common premise.

jokes.text: Most folks just call me orange Joe.
jokes.setup: Professor admits he doesn't know his crew's names and asks what they call the character
jokes.punchline: The obvious nickname based on his orange color
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-specific humor. The obviousness is the joke.

jokes.text: It's July, right? Let's wait three months and go to Oktoberfest!
jokes.setup: Professor suggests a social outing for the crew
jokes.punchline: His bizarre logic of waiting months instead of going to a current event
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth logic - technically correct but absurdly inefficient.

jokes.text: Hey! I don't smell bavarian-style vomit. Where's Oktoberfest?
jokes.setup: Characters arrive expecting traditional Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: Fry's expectation that vomit is a defining characteristic of the festival
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity - 'bavarian-style vomit' is both disgusting and hilariously precise.

jokes.text: No, I wouldn't want to ruin my Oktoberfest by becoming intoxicated.
jokes.setup: Characters at what should be a beer-heavy festival
jokes.punchline: Someone refusing alcohol at Oktoberfest to preserve the 'authentic' experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - missing the point of Oktoberfest entirely while claiming to preserve it.

jokes.text: You can all act like Jersey shore socialites, but at least Bender will party with me.
jokes.setup: Fry frustrated with the refined Oktoberfest atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Comparing sophisticated behavior to Jersey Shore socialites (backwards comparison)
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference feels dated and the logic is confusing rather than funny.

jokes.text: I enjoy partying as much as the next fellow, but this is Oktoberfest. Good day, sir!
jokes.setup: Fry expects Bender to party with him
jokes.punchline: Bender suddenly becomes pretentious and refined, abandoning Fry
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion - Bender acting dignified is always funny.

jokes.text: Ach du freakin' lieber!
jokes.setup: Fry's frustration at being abandoned
jokes.punchline: Mixing German exclamation with modern profanity substitute
jokes.explanation: Clever linguistic joke mixing authentic German with cartoon-friendly profanity.

jokes.text: Shut up! I said it first!
jokes.setup: Fry makes the 'fun on a bun' pun
jokes.punchline: Elzar's childish claim that he thought of it first
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for celebrity chef Elzar - petty and ego-driven.

jokes.text: I've just recently started always having dreamt of being a sausage-making champion.
jokes.setup: Fry wants to join the contest
jokes.punchline: His tortured grammar trying to sound like he's always wanted this
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mangling of English that perfectly captures Fry's impulsive nature and poor grammar.

jokes.text: He's only using the choicest cuts of hummingbird.
jokes.setup: Elzar explains the competition's exotic ingredients
jokes.punchline: Using tiny hummingbirds as luxury meat
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd escalation of fancy ingredients - hummingbirds are comically tiny for sausage.

jokes.text: Murakami-San got his hands on a nice rack of angel wings. Gonna grind 'em up into heavenwurst.
jokes.setup: Continuing the exotic ingredient list
jokes.punchline: Angel wings as meat plus 'heavenwurst' pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to literally divine ingredients, plus clever 'heavenwurst' wordplay.

jokes.text: Pork that's been aged for 3,000 years!
jokes.setup: Elzar reveals he's also using pork after claiming others need exotic meat
jokes.punchline: The absurd aging time that makes regular pork special
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - after exotic ingredients, regular pork with impossible aging.

jokes.text: Aw, man! I can't beat that with a Craigslist pig.
jokes.setup: Fry realizes his regular ingredients are inadequate
jokes.punchline: The implication that he got his pig from Craigslist
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's cheap, sketchy sourcing vs. exotic ingredients.

jokes.text: I'll have to kill you later for some other reason.
jokes.setup: Elzar apologizing to Fry for the competitive disadvantage
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning future murder plans as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivery - treating murder as a mundane task to be rescheduled.

jokes.text: Man, all the fun has been taken out of this once-noble barf-a-palooza.
jokes.setup: Fry lamenting the refined nature of modern Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: Calling traditional Oktoberfest a 'noble barf-a-palooza'
jokes.explanation: Excellent oxymoron combining 'noble' with 'barf-a-palooza' - perfectly captures Fry's perspective.

jokes.text: Well, at least we were hammered!
jokes.setup: Leela calls people in Fry's time primitive and crude
jokes.punchline: Fry's proud defense that at least they were drunk
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Fry's values perfectly inverted from modern sophistication.

jokes.text: Fahrvergnügen! Kaiser permanente! Fru... frusen gladje!
jokes.setup: Fry drunkenly celebrating
jokes.punchline: Shouting random German-sounding words that are actually brand names/non-German
jokes.explanation: Clever mix of fake German - Fahrvergnügen (VW), Kaiser Permanente (health insurer), Frusen Gladje (ice cream).

jokes.text: Hey, come on, you keister-meisters! Eins, zwei, drei, vier! Lift your Stein and drink your beer! Ich bin ein never!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to get others to join his celebration
jokes.punchline: Mangled German including 'keister-meisters' and 'Ich bin ein never'
jokes.explanation: Mixing butt joke with German plus JFK reference mangled - typical Fry linguistic chaos.

jokes.text: Even with harps, that's still the famous Oktoberfest chicken dance!
jokes.setup: Characters doing what appears to be an embarrassing dance
jokes.punchline: Fancy instrumentation doesn't change the fundamental silliness of the chicken dance
jokes.explanation: Good observation about how refinement can't disguise inherent silliness.

jokes.text: Get das Booty up here, Leela! I brought his-and-her chicken hats!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to get Leela to join his celebration
jokes.punchline: Mixing German 'das' with 'booty' plus the absurd chicken hats
jokes.explanation: Somewhat forced German/slang mix, but the chicken hats add visual absurdity.

jokes.text: You are a boorish, primitive neanderthal! We were going out?!
jokes.setup: Leela angrily breaks up with Fry calling him a Neanderthal
jokes.punchline: Fry's complete surprise that they were dating
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with great character work - Fry's obliviousness is genuine and funny.

jokes.text: Whoo! I mean, nooo!
jokes.setup: Fry just learned he was being broken up with
jokes.punchline: His immediate celebratory reaction followed by attempted correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Fry's honest reaction then social awareness kicking in.

jokes.text: ...and-and the worst part is, I had to have the breakup sex by myself!
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about the breakup
jokes.punchline: The implication of what 'breakup sex by myself' means
jokes.explanation: Adult humor handled with characteristic Futurama blend of crude and innocent.

jokes.text: It's supposed to be beer and bratwurst, not breakups and... Boring thing!
jokes.setup: Fry lamenting what Oktoberfest has become
jokes.punchline: His attempt at alliteration failing with 'boring thing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - trying to be clever with wordplay but giving up mid-attempt.

jokes.text: Mammoth meat aged for 30,000 years? Prize-winning sausage meat, here I come!
jokes.setup: Documentary mentions perfectly preserved mammoths aged 30,000 years
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately sees the connection to the sausage contest's aged meat theme
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot connection and character moment - Bender's scheming nature perfectly utilized.

jokes.text: This is the elephant detector. I just set it to 'big and woolly.'
jokes.setup: Fry questions if the ship has a mammoth detector
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact explanation of repurposing the elephant detector
jokes.explanation: Great future-tech absurdism with perfect deadpan delivery from Leela.

jokes.text: ♪ Sausage is great, sausage is great ♪ ♪ Sausage, sausage, sausage... ♪
jokes.setup: Bender operating the meat grinder
jokes.punchline: His simple, repetitive song celebrating sausage
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment - Bender's enthusiasm expressed through basic song.

jokes.text: Last I saw, he was jumping up and down in my meat grinder.
jokes.setup: Characters looking for Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender casually mentions Fry was in the meat grinder
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy setup with Bender's characteristic casual attitude toward Fry's potential death.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! That looks like Fry's hair!
jokes.setup: Characters eating the sausage and enjoying it
jokes.punchline: Discovery of what appears to be Fry's hair in the food
jokes.explanation: Classic horror-comedy reveal - the evidence of what they've unknowingly done.

jokes.text: It's tragedy on a bun.
jokes.setup: Characters discovering Fry appears to be dead
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier 'fun on a bun' but with tragic twist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms the earlier innocent pun into dark comedy. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: I broke up with my boyfriend and then I ate him!
jokes.setup: Leela's horror at the situation
jokes.punchline: The absurd sequence of events stated plainly
jokes.explanation: Perfect summary of the absurd situation with great escalation of guilt.

jokes.text: Oh, now, now, we've all been there.
jokes.setup: Leela's horror at eating her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual dismissal as if it's common
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally dark comedy delivered with perfect Professor Farnsworth casual dismissal. Implies disturbing universal experience.

jokes.text: He thought he recognized one of the monkeys!
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining a memory being removed
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous reason Fry fell off the zoo train
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - completely plausible character moment that's absurd to anyone else.

jokes.text: Geez, who died?
jokes.setup: Fry returns to find everyone somber
jokes.punchline: His casual question when he literally died (or appeared to)
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Fry unknowingly asks about his own death with classic casual delivery.

jokes.text: Maybe we should hire a delivery boy. You mean like: Fry?
jokes.setup: Leela suggests hiring a delivery boy since they're short-staffed
jokes.punchline: Bender's obvious suggestion of Fry, who's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic moment, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Nah, I'm just kidding. My pastor helped me through my grief.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming Fry was his best friend and he's dead
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing it was a joke and mentioning his pastor
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - fake emotion followed by unexpected religious reference.

jokes.text: Look normal to me.
jokes.setup: Fry says he hit his head pretty bad
jokes.punchline: Neanderthal's assessment that Fry's damaged state looks normal
jokes.explanation: Good insult humor about Fry's intelligence from prehistoric perspective.

jokes.text: Anyone dumb enough get stuck in ice probably neanderthal.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he's one of them
jokes.punchline: The logic that stupidity equals Neanderthal heritage
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to earlier Neanderthal insult with reasonable logic from their perspective.

jokes.text: 53 tons of hamburgers and French frr... agments of not potatoes.
jokes.setup: Describing a delivery to avoid mentioning Fry
jokes.punchline: Catching herself saying 'French fries' and awkwardly correcting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's attempt to avoid Fry-related words.

jokes.text: They get a big shipment every fri... Saturday!
jokes.setup: Continuing to describe delivery schedule
jokes.punchline: Another near-mention of 'Friday' quickly corrected
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation - functional but less impact than first instance.

jokes.text: And I do have vague memories of people refusing to breed with me!
jokes.setup: Fry learning about Neanderthal persecution
jokes.punchline: His recognition that this matches his own romantic history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry self-awareness moment connecting prehistoric persecution to his dating life.

jokes.text: Maybe someone look into it, many years from now.
jokes.setup: Discovering hole melted in ice that leads outside
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of urgent escape opportunity
jokes.explanation: Mild bureaucratic humor about procrastination, even in prehistoric times.

jokes.text: those stuck-up homo sapiens with their tools and their pants and such.
jokes.setup: Gugg rallying against modern humans
jokes.punchline: Listing basic civilizational advances as pretentious affectations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from tools to pants - treating basic clothing as snobbery.

jokes.text: This is the greatest injustice Germany has ever committed!
jokes.setup: Bender's sausage only wins third place
jokes.punchline: Comparing sausage contest results to Germany's actual historical injustices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - completely disproportionate response that ignores actual German history. Classic Futurama boundary-pushing.

jokes.text: Dear God, look at those hairy elephantés!
jokes.setup: Neanderthals attacking the festival
jokes.punchline: Zapp's confused identification of mammoths as 'hairy elephants' with affected pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp moment - pretentious but incorrect, with his characteristic fake sophistication.

jokes.text: Then how are they unmanned? The men are just for ballast.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining their 'unmanned' drones that have men in them
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical explanation that the men are just weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp logic - confident explanation that makes no sense but sounds military-ish.

jokes.text: it's designed to withstand even the weight of a modern-day elephant foot.
jokes.setup: Zapp boasting about his control unit's durability
jokes.punchline: Specific resistance to elephant foot, just before mammoth attack
jokes.explanation: Good setup for imminent destruction - overly specific boast about the exact thing that will defeat it.

jokes.text: We want our land back! Also, to be treated as equals! And a little mating wouldn't hurt!
jokes.setup: Neanderthal demands in their war
jokes.punchline: Escalation from political demands to personal romantic needs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from serious political demands to personal needs, with nice callback.

jokes.text: You want make love, not war? Jawohl! This change everything, tight-clothes-man.
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela's romantic reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Neanderthal's interpretation as literal 'make love not war' solution
jokes.explanation: Clever use of classic peace slogan as literal solution, with good character voice.

jokes.text: Chef Fritz accidentally got thrown down an icy slope! I'm the sausage champion!
jokes.setup: Bender celebrating his victory
jokes.punchline: Winning by default due to opponent's 'accidental' demise
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - celebrating victory while casually mentioning suspicious circumstances.

jokes.text: Just this once, I'm going to let you embarrass me.
jokes.setup: Fry inviting Leela to dance
jokes.punchline: Framing dancing with her as allowing embarrassment rather than romance
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with typical Fry backwards romantic logic.

tmdb_overview: Fry is the victim of a sausage-making accident during the crew's visit to Oktoberfest.
